Revision 02 - 1-Previously building classification was submitted as F2. The proposed building is a Valve House, unoccupied space that is built to provide closed room for valves. Lift the Hold application related to a new 10-storey building, containing 80 emergency shelter beds, 100 transitional shelter beds, 124 long-term care beds, 70 supportive housing units, and a 2,269 square metre community hub. The proposal would replace the existing Seaton House men's shelter. For more information about the proposal, go to: www.toronto.ca/george-street-revitalization

Official Plan Amendment and Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit a 30-storey building with residential and commercial uses. The building proposes 351 residential units. Vehicle access is from Greenbriar Road, leading to a three level parking garage, containing 97 parking spaces, including 5 parking spaces shared between visitors and the commercial uses. A total of 274 bicycle parking spaces are proposed including 239 long term and 35 short term spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 22,416.70 square metres and a FSI of 11.23. Additionally, the proposed development provides 637.90 square metres of indoor amenity space and 632.40 square metres of outdoor amenity space.

In addition, the amendment seeks to increase the required indoor and outdoor amenity space while reducing the green roof area and overall coverage. These changes are intended to refine the development while maintaining the overall intent and character of the original approval.

Zoning By-Law Amendment and Site Plan Control application to permit a mixed-use building with two towers, 32 and 46 storeys (113.3 metres and 155.2 metres) in height, connected by an 11-storey podium. District plan, zoning by-law amendment and draft plan of subdivision applications for Taxiway District, which comprises 40.7 hectares (100.7 acres) of land consisting of hangars, offices and ancillary structures, surface parking lots, and a portion of the former Downsview Airport taxiway. Collectively, the district is proposed to accommodate approximately 489,009 square metres of GFA (282,750 square metres non-residential GFA and 206,259 square metres residential GFA) with a resulting gross density of 1.2 FSI. The proposed development will provide approximately 7,400 jobs and 2,957 dwelling units. The proposed development will maintain a predominantly mid-scale built form character with an option for two tall buildings of 19 and 23 storeys. Adaptive reuse of buildings from the previous aerospace manufacturing operations will accommodate a range of new institutional, retail, entertainment, and community uses. A range of housing options are proposed including affordable housing. 930 square metres of GFA are proposed for community uses along with a 1,547 square metre child care facility (1,001m indoor; 546 outdoor). The district plan proposes 14,295 square metres of public parkland across 3 parks, 13,844 square metres of privately-owned publicly accessible spaces, and 9,103 square metres of greenways.

Zoning By-law Amendment application for Phases 4 & 5 of the Regent Park redevelopment. The Phases 4 & 5 lands are generally bounded by Gerrard Street to the north, River Street to the east, Oak Street to the south, and Dreamers Way to the west. The proposal consists of a total gross floor area (GFA) of 320,064 m2 comprised of approximately 303,616 m2 of residential GFA and approximately 16,452 m2 of non-residential GFA across 13 buildings (including a new Toronto Public Library branch relocated from Parliament Street).
